memory.summary: The leisure option looks tempting right now, but my project is barely started. I need to ignore the relaxation and focus on that quick chore instead.

desires: Get that trivial chore done immediately so I can check it off and feel a quick win without spending too much time on it.
Avoid talking to my close friends tonight; I don't want to waste energy or risk an awkward conversation when I'm already tired.
Procrastinate on the term project just a little longer since the deadline is still three days away and the work feels soft enough to wait on.

goals.description: Complete the trivial quick_chore immediately to get a dopamine hit and clear my head.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference and high procrastination bias mean I can't wait on the big project, but this tiny task offers instant relief without requiring deep focus. It satisfies my need for a 'quick win'.

goals.description: Decline social interaction with close friends tonight to conserve energy and avoid potential awkwardness.
goals.rationale: I'm already running on low sleep debt and stress. My avoidant conflict style makes me wary of friction, and my current value weighting puts relationships lower than immediate comfort when I'm tired.

goals.description: Do absolutely nothing on the term project today; it's due in three days and feels soft enough to ignore for now.
goals.rationale: With a short planning horizon, the deadline feels distant. The 'soft' nature of the work combined with my risk tolerance means I'll likely underestimate how much is needed until it becomes urgent.

agent.last_reasoning: I need the instant dopamine hit to clear my head before the afternoon drag sets in. Skipping this 'trivial' task feels like wasted potential, even if I ignore the term project. It's a low-effort win that satisfies my achievement drive without demanding deep focus.
